mnemonics.title: HDLC Frame Structure
mnemonics.content:
F A C I FCS F
F = Flag (01111110)
A = Address (who)
C = Control (what type)
I = Information (data)
FCS = Frame Check Sequence
F = Flag (repeat)

mnemonics.title: S-Frame Types
mnemonics.content:
RR = Ready to Receive (Positive ACK)
RNR = Not Ready (Pause/Buffer full)
REJ = Reject (Go-back-N)
SREJ = Selective Reject (Selective Repeat)

mnemonics.title: U-Frame Commands
mnemonics.content:
SABM - Set Asynchronous Balanced Mode (Connect)
DISC - Disconnect
UA - Unnumbered Acknowledgment
DM - Disconnected Mode (Can't connect)
FRMR - Frame Reject (Error)

acronyms.usage: Bit-oriented protocol for synchronous serial communication
Carries user data with sequence numbers
Flow control and error recovery (RR, RNR, REJ)
Secondary transmits only when polled by primary
Command (P) from primary, Response (F) from secondary
Mathematical error detection method used in FCS

visualTips.visualization: Draw a rectangle divided into 6 sections: Flag | Address | Control | Information | FCS | Flag. Use arrows to show bit stuffing occurring before FCS, not in flag or address/control.
Show a flowchart: After 5 consecutive 1s in data → insert 0 → continue. Receiver removes 0 after 5 consecutive 1s. Example: 01101111101111101 → becomes 011011111001111101 after stuffing.
Draw three states: Disconnected → Establishing → Data Transfer → Disconnecting. Show SABM → UA moves to Established; DISC → UA moves to Disconnected.
Show rotating modulo-8 (or 128) counter. N(S) = send sequence, N(R) = receive sequence. Illustrate window of up to 7 unacknowledged frames.
Primary sends frame with P=1 → Secondary must respond with F=1. Show timing diagram with arrows labeled 'Poll' and 'Final'.

analogies.analogy: Like a postal letter: Flag = envelope flaps (start/end), Address = recipient name, Control = letter type (bill, letter, postcard), Information = the message, FCS = seal verification.
Like adding pauses in speech to prevent confusion with a code word. If you need to say the code word '11111', you insert a pause (0) to break it up.
RR = 'Got it, send more', RNR = 'Hold on, buffer full', REJ = 'Oops, start from that one again'. Like a receiver giving feedback to sender.
NRM = Teacher-student (teacher calls on students), ABM = Business meeting (anyone can speak when they have something to say).
Primary = Radio tower, Secondaries = Walkie-talkies. Tower controls who can transmit by saying 'Unit 1, go ahead' (polling).
